ops.body:
# The launchctl install sequences (they differ between the two agents)

Both start with a best-effort **bootout** of any previous instance (so bootstrap does not conflict; idempotent install) and end with **plain kickstart (no -k)** — it covers the loaded-but-not-running race, no-ops when already running, and never cold-starts a second copy (`-k` would). The **enable placement differs**:

- `service.AppKeepAlive.Install`: bootout → **checked enable → bootstrap** → kickstart. Enable runs BEFORE bootstrap because it clears a user/MDM disable regardless of load state, and a disabled label fails bootstrap before enable could self-heal.
- `service.Agent.Install`: bootout → **bootstrap → best-effort enable** → kickstart.

`notLoaded`: bootout exits 3 ("No such process") for an unloaded target — not an error on uninstall.

# AppKeepAlive: /usr/bin/open -g -W

The agent's Program is `open -g -W <app>`: -W makes open BLOCK until the app exits AND attach to an already-running instance rather than launching a second copy — so launchd's KeepAlive relaunches the app only on a real exit and never spins against a live holder (-g keeps the launch backgrounded). Bootout kills only the blocked open waiter, never the app it waits on — which is why Uninstall leaves the app running, and why Install over a running app attaches instead of double-starting. A bootout failure other than "not loaded" aborts before the plist is removed, so a still-loaded agent never becomes an on-disk-invisible orphan. A private, cask-less holder binary is not a bundle open can adopt this way; it stays CLI-spawned (proc.Spawn) with no LaunchAgent.

# RestartPolicy (7623022)

Restart behavior is a required typed field on both agents (service/restart.go: RestartAlways / RestartOnFailure / NoRestart) selecting the rendered plist's restart keys, instead of a hardcoded KeepAlive block per agent. The zero value is invalid on purpose — a consumer must choose.

# Why a user LaunchAgent, and brew reconciliation

Per-user agent, not a root daemon, so the daemon can reach the user's login Keychain. Agent.Program deliberately skips EvalSymlinks: a Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin symlink stays a constant launchd program path across `brew upgrade` instead of churning per Cellar path. IsBrewManaged inspects the executable path only; brew-managed daemons are driven via `brew services`, and BrewKickstart exists because `brew services start` only bootstraps the job — a stop/start bootout race can leave it loaded-but-never-running (RunAtLoad fires only at bootstrap). Plist values are XML-escaped because <, >, & are legal in APFS paths; env keys render sorted so the plist is reproducible; keepalive's AssociatedBundleIdentifiers block is omitted entirely when empty so the rendered plist stays byte-identical to the donor's no-bundle output.
